Tests for the `Math.max` function with various numeric inputs, including NaN and Infinity.

# Purpose
This code is a test suite for the `Math.max` function in JavaScript. It uses assertions to verify that `Math.max` correctly identifies the maximum value among its arguments, including special cases involving `NaN`, `Infinity`, and `-Infinity`. The tests ensure that when `NaN` is an argument, the result is `NaN`, and when `Infinity` is present, it is returned as the maximum value. The code also checks the behavior of `Math.max` with zero and negative zero, confirming that the result is positive zero. Additionally, it verifies that when no arguments are provided, `Math.max` returns `-Infinity`. This script provides narrow functionality focused on validating the behavior of the `Math.max` function under various conditions.
